title: GREAT watch for a LOW price!
text: I originally purchased this watch 2 years ago for my daughter.  She really likes it.  This year I purchased one for myself.  It's terrific!  Lightweight and durable.  The VeryFitPro app is great and works well.  I can receive email, text, call, FB, Twitter notifications and more (my daughter says it works with Snapchat as well). Biometrics are accurate.  Has a menstrual cycle reminder, which is great. Screen size is adequate and easy to read.  Very easy to set up.  It also has a "relax" feature that is triggered by high heart rate, or you can just use it when you need to do some breathing exercises.  Battery life is really strong. I highly recommend this watch.

title: LOVE this watch!!
text: I was super skeptical about getting this watch… but it’s perfect!!! It tracks (if you allow it on app!) steps, heart rate, calories burned, sleep, mileage walked, how long active… can view it on watch or app. It also views messages fully, & will alert you if you’re getting a call. Can also use it as a back up alarm! You can’t reply back to messages on this. You can ignore a call but not accept a call on it. It’s a good option if you can’t/won’t buy the smartwatches! The battery life has lasted me 7 days on a full charge!!

title: Perfect Smart Watch- Buy This Over Apple Watch
text: I have had an Apple Watch in the past and I hated it. The app circles on the home screen were too overwhelming and cluttered. It did more than I needed it to. I ended up giving it to my stepdad about a year ago, because I wasn’t going to wear it. Fast forward to a year later and I’m looking for something that tells time, shows heart rate, tracks sleep, and is a step counter. But simplified. I found everything I needed in this watch. The interface is clean. It’s easy to navigate. The information loads quickly into the mobile app and the app isn’t cluttered. It’s simple, it’s helpful, it’s great. And it’s waterproof, so if it gets wet when I’m washing my hands 100 times a day, if a student spills something on me, or if I accidentally forget to take it off before a shower, it doesn’t matter. It’s all good. And I did get it wet- no problem. I also love that it’s right around $30. If it gets scratched or broken while I’m teaching or something, I’m not going to be overly upset because it was

title: Battery really DOES last a long time
text: The watch does what it states it will do but I wanted to reiterate that the battery really DOES last a long time.  I've had it on for over a week and the battery is still at 42%.  I feel like I probably check it a bit more than someone who has had one for longer because it's still novel.  I really like it and it gives enough detail for me, although others might want more detail in the app.  I love the way the watch looks.  It's easy to read and very comfortable.  I love seeing notifications for my texts and emails without going to my phone.  Overall...good product if you don't want to spend a lot of money.

title: BT notifications rarely work
text: [[VIDEOID:51a2d6f0b114989e519a57ab1da826c7]] I bought this in mid August. I like waiting a couple months before I leave reviews because of proper usage. The battery life for this watch is excellent! I love that it has 3 different face features to view on your watch. However, there's some features that have been inconsistent since I bought the watch. Bluetooth for example, it would disconnect from my phone randomly, solution was to refresh the device on the app. This worked for a couple months. By late November it stopped working completely, it's connected to the app and it logs my steps, sometimes my heart rate but no longer notifies me of incoming calls and texts which was the sole purpose of it for me. App says it's active and notifications are turned on, but I don't get any. So that's my beef. Also the band has lost its color over the months too, that doesn't bother me but I know it can for others.

title: Updated review
text:
Terrible. The touchscreen doesn’t work, I could hardly get it to turn on.

*update*
I returned the defective device and The seller was very responsive in wanting to fix the issue. My new watch has arrived and is much better! It linked with my phone quickly and seems to be functioning appropriately as far as tracking steps and heart rate. The band is a little irritating to my skin but it looks very nice. I have looked into replacement bands though and they are available. Overall happy with the replacement.

title: It's just not from me.
text:
[[VIDEOID:ebe90a4cdd6b9e1fdaa06c233e90bc60]] Updae! The seller has been very good about making sure i was satisfied witn tje watch.
Update! Battery life is great been using it 3 days on one charge. Won't stay connected to my phone I have to keep going back in and manually reconnecting it. So still not happy with it. I gave it a chance but again it's not for me!
It's very cheaply made and you can tell! Definitely not happy with it! You can receive messages and stuff on it but again you can tell it's china made. I will be returning it and buying a better one! I had to plug it into the charger for it to even power on. Set up was easy and worked well but it's not not for me

title: Wanted to like this watch but....
text: Took a star off because the step count is way low compared to 3 other trackers that ALL read the same until this one. The other star I deducted was due to the band. The leg the band attach to are 19.7 mm instead of 20 mm and the legs are tapered slightly which means for most persons this band would not be replaceable. You have to file down, and use the pins from the old band which was difficult, but not impossible. The app is good,Veryfitpro, but for some reason they removed the ability to adjust the stride length which would have corrected the discrepancy in the step count. I can't recommend this watch. Not returning, but displeased.

title: Don't scan QR Code!
text:
I scanned the code and it wanted credit card info - it only accepted Visa and it rejected mine. I went to Google play and downloaded the app. Then I got 2 charges on the visa:
03/18 2.99 ORRMVBFNV DOT COM
03/18 39.99 NFTWCHR.COM
My bank caught it and said sometimes a glitch will happen and switch you to another website but I'm not sure if they were hacked or it was an accident.
I've had it for a week now and love it. I took a trip to las vegas and was shocked by how many steps I took! Doesn't seem to have blood presssure measurement which is disappointing. I don't need it as a phone but I like that it tells me when I get a message or email and from whom. I don't like the band but the phone is worth buying another band for it.

title: Best of the cheapos
text: I have bought and unhappily returned many "smart" watches under $50 (almost all made in China).  This is the only one that I feel satisfies my needs.  All I want is to see the time, date, and day when I raise my wrist, and vibrate on calls and texts.  I don't use any of the other functions, so I'm happy with this watch.

title: hard to set up with app
text: had trouble connecting the app to get the watch set up; instructions were not helpful; overall, it seems to work fine

title: Purchased in late August, broken by mid October
text:
This watch was the closest replacement for my wonderful smartwatch purchase on Amazon a couple years ago that I unfortunately fried by submerging in water after a particularly messy surgery.

The product worked great, although with less battery life than my previous watch (about a week on this one vs 10 full days on my previous watch), and menus were slightly more cumbersome, but manageable.

Unfortunately, it's less than 2 months later, and the button on the side has completely stopped working. I've tried resetting the watch, unlinking it from the app, turning the watch off for prolonged periods...nothing works, it's just broken. Thankfully, I have the watch set to wrist sense, so it still functions, for now, but the on-screen buttons are easy to accidentally push and turn features on and off, and once I accidentally turn wrist sense off, the watch will no longer be functional.

This is a piece of junk, so look elsewhere for your smartwatch needs!

title: So far, so good
text:
I have only had it for one day. I am transitioning from a Fitbit brand so getting used to the differences. I really like the screen size and brightness as well as the feel of the band. If the band wears out over time I hope replacements are available.
It was not iverly difficult to set up for me and that was a good thing.

title: It stopped working after 6 days
text: I got the watch 6 days ago and it just randomly stopped working.. wont charge or come on. This is sad because I really like this watch.

title: stopped working almost immediately
text: Looks nice but started having problems within first week of use.

title: Great value
text: This is a good watch if you don’t want to spend a lot of money on a smart watch. I like only charging it every other week or so. I get notifications for texts and calls but do have to synchronize more than I would like to the app.

title: It Is Not Waterproof!
text: Got it for my son. He had it on while taking a quick bath. Got it wet and when I got it from him to check on it, since it was wet and I had no idea if it would work or not, it was pretty much ruined. When I tried to get it to work, it did what it wanted to do instead of letting us do what we wanted to do with it. So, I do not recommend it unless you can use it with no water.

title: Good for an estimate of activity, but not accurate.  Also loses 2-3 mins a week if not synced.
text:
This is a basic watch that has some flaws.

Pros:

> Easy to read large numbers for the time
> Simple to swipe between screens to access information
> excellent battery life
> heart rate monitor appears accurate
> can notify you of incoming texts when linked to your phone (if you have a device that is supported)

Cons:

> Loses 2-3 minutes a week if not synced regularly with the app. It’s very annoying when the watch says it’s 9:57am when it is really 10:00am. It’s made me late to work a few times!
> Sometimes records sitting in a moving vehicle, especially in slow moving traffic, as exercise.

Summary:

If you want accuracy, this is probably not the watch for you. If you need a basic idea of how much you’re moving, don’t spend time in slow traffic, and can remember to sync it regularly to the app to keep the time accurate, then it’s an affordable alternative to name brand fitness watches.

title: It works great 👍
text: [[VIDEOID:fc88e8ed994b7c537f4dba54bd010c27]] I used it daily

title: Heart Rate Monitor
text: I’m a little disappointed with the watch. I bought it for the main reason of tracking my heart rate as I exercise and it tends to be pretty inaccurate not just when I’m exercising. I’ll use an actual blood pressure monitor to check and it’ll say my heart rate is in the 70s and my watch will be all over the place from 40s to 109 to 80s. I’ve tried readjusting it on my wrist several times to make sure I’m wearing it correctly but it’s just not reliable in that area. Other than that it’s a nice watch to track my steps and check the time or calls.

title: Smart Watch
text: This watch is (so far) the best one I have found.  I only have to charge it about every 3-4 days.  It keeps perfect time, connects to my cpap app well.

title: LOVE
text:
First the battery life on this thing is amazing!

Second I just love it! It does everything but answer calls and texts.

title: Ok product
text: Nice watch ok if you don't need alot of apps. Doesn't carry alot of features just simple everyday tasks, the only think I dislike about the watch which was why I gave it a 4 start os because of the watch band, get felt very flimsy and cheap as if they would pop off my hand in seconds, but iverall for a single Android watch this product is okay, it all depends on the features you would be looking for.

title: Almost perfect
text: For the price this has to be the best heart rate monitor/smart watch I've used. I can't speak for all the features as I really only use it for the heart rate monitor which seems incredibly accurate. The sport feature for bike riding is reasonably accurate. The sport feature for walking is not accurate for me using a treadmill. It's close but not 100%. Heart rate is 100% accurate. I don't use the phone features at all but I know it has a phone notification but you cannot answer on the watch. I've chosen to switch the phone features off. The app keeps a nice record of my workouts. Good buy for the price.

title: mistaken for Apple Watch; I love it!
text: I really think I'm getting my 29.00 dollars worth. I can track my sleep and my exercise , my steps! and it reminds me to get up and move! The heart rate and blood pressure parts seem fairly spot on. I don't think I understand exactly how to use parts of it, but it did what I wanted. :)

title: Nice watch/step counter
text: I like the watch. The numbers are large and easy to read. Has an alarm, counts steps, exercise timer, tracks your heart rate and sleep. Excellent price. The only thing I didn't like was the band. Way too large for my wrist. Bought an adjustable one and thew the one that came with it away.

title: Great inexpensive smart watch.
text: I have been wanting a smart watch but to cheap to pay hundreds of dollars. This does everything I need and the price is right.

title: I love the watch
text: Using it for walking tracking my steps
